query_normalized: llmxive follow-up: extending "memory is reconstructed, not retrieved: graph memory for llm agents" computer science
rationale: This paper satisfies criteria (a) and (b) as it directly addresses the core mechanism of "graph memory" (a form of externalized memory) for LLM agents, which is the specific topic of the user's follow-up research. Although it is a review rather than a specific graph-implementation study, it establishes the canonical framework and empirical context for the "Memory is Reconstructed" concept the user is extending.
